title: Iranians around the world celebrate killing of Ayatollah Khamenei | ABC NEWS
description: Iranians have rallied around the world to celebrate the death of the country’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei after the US and Israel launched a bombardment operation against the Islamic regime. Iranian diaspora have been seen waving pre-1979 Iranian flags and singing pro-Shah chants in major cities such as London, Los Angeles and Melbourne.  Many anti-regime demonstrators were praising US President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netnayahu’s move. title: 'More than 100 children' dead in US and Israel strike on Iranian school, Iran says | ABC NEWS
description: Iran's ambassador to the United Nations has accused the US and Israel of targeting the school and other civilian population centres. "The number of innocent civilians continues to rise. This is not only an act of aggression, it is a war crime, and a crime against humanity. "The aggressors have also targeted a school in the city of Minab … killing more than 100 children," Amir-Saeid Iravani told an emergency meeting of the UN Security Council. The US and Israel have not immediately responded to Iran's claims. The figures from Iranian authorities have not been independently verified by the ABC. title: Who was Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei? | ABC NEWS
description: Iranian state media has confirmed the country's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been killed. The announcement follows a statement from US President Donald Trump on social media, claiming Khamenei was killed in a joint US and Israeli air strike. Khamenei ruled Iran for more than 35 years, cracking down on liberal values and promoting religious conservatism. He strengthened Iran's military and built a network of proxy groups in the region to counter the influence of the United States and Israel. title: Iranian strikes damage Dubai international airport | ABC NEWS
description: Dubai's international airport and its landmark Burj Al Arab hotel have been damaged in Iran's retaliatory attacks targeting the Gulf states and the wider Middle East. The airport's media office said four staff were injured ‌on Sunday but most passengers had left the area before the strike. Dubai and Abu Dhabi have been impacted by Iran's retaliatory strikes, including hotels as well as the cities' airports. One person died at the Abu Dhabi airport. The airspace over the UAE remains closed, disrupting global travel on a massive scale.  Reporting via ABC/Reuters/AFP/AP.
